name: Appen AI Data Annotation Platform (ADAP) API
description:
# Introduction

The Appen AI Data Annotation Platform (ADAP) APIs offer a structured, automated approach to ensure high-quality data labeling on a scale. These APIs facilitate seamless integration of quality assurance steps, including reviewer assignments, and performance scoring, into annotation workflows. 

By standardizing quality processes and enabling programmatic control, they reduce errors, improve data consistency, and provide reliable training data for AI and machine learning models. 

Before integrating with the API, we recommend building and running a job using our **[graphical user interface](https://client.appen.com/sessions/new)**. Once you successfully run a job and are satisfied with the results, you can automate the process using the Appen API.

# Authentication

To authenticate and access the API, follow these steps to find it:

**Obtain Your API Key:**

- Click your profile picture in the top right corner of any page, then select Profile from the dropdown.

![](https://your-bucket-name.s3.amazonaws.com/images/profile-picture-location.png)   
![](https://qualityflow-api-docs.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/ADAP/authentication1.png)   

- Go to the API tab and click Generate New to create an API key.

![](https://your-bucket-name.s3.amazonaws.com/images/api-tab-location.png)    
![](https://qualityflow-api-docs.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/ADAP/APIKey-Screenshot.png)    

**Note:** Creating a new API key will invalidate any previous keys for this user, so update any code that uses the old key.

- For requests listed below, the API Key will be listed as `{api_key}`.    

- Include the API key in the header for each request as follows:

```
headers: { 

  "AUTHORIZATION": "Token token={api_key}"

}
```

**Other Variables:**

- `{job_id}` and `{team_id}` can be found on the job listing page or the Teams tab URL on the Teams Tab Account page.

![](https://qualityflow-api-docs.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/ADAP/TeamId.png)

![](https://qualityflow-api-docs.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/ADAP/jobId.png)

# Resources

There are four primary data objects on Appen that correspond to resources on the API:

## Projects

A **Project** is a container that organizes and structures your data annotation workflows. 

Projects serve as the foundation for organizing related jobs, units, and data within your team. 

Each project belongs to a specific team and can contain multiple jobs.

Projects have unique names that help you organize and identify different annotation initiatives. Think of a project as a workspace where you can group related annotation tasks, manage data uploads, and coordinate quality assurance workflows across multiple jobs.


## Jobs

A **Job** is an interface that connects your data to an online workforce. Each job on the Appen platform has data, instructions, customizable questions for your use case (written in CML), [test questions](https://success.appen.com/hc/en-us/articles/202702985-Guide-to-Creating-Test-Questions), and is worked on by Contributors. Contributors submit Judgments on the rows of data via a worker interface.

- All jobs in a single account can be found on your project canvas page and will be identified by a unique numeric id.

## Units (also known as 'Rows')

A **Unit** of data is uploaded to the job from your source data file via API or GUI.

## Judgments

A **Judgment** is the set of responses submitted by a contributor on a particular unit of data. It is recommended to collect multiple judgments and compare them to one another or aggregate to the top response. For each job, you can specify the number of judgments you would like each row to receive.

# Responses and Messaging

Each request to the Appen API returns an HTTP status code response and one or both of the following:

- An application response message (in JSON)
- A JSON representation of the object (job, unit, or judgments) requested See the below sections of different API Requests, Responses and Messaging for more information:

1. **[Project Creation](#operation/createProject)**
2. **[Upload Dataset](#operation/uploadDatasetToProject)**
3. **[Upload Dataset Status](#operation/getUploadStatus)**
4. **[Job Creation](#operation/createJob)**
5. **[Copy Job](#operation/copyJob)**
6. **[Job Status](#operation/getJobStatus)**
7. **[Update Job Settings](#operation/updateJob)**
8. **[List Units in the Project](#operation/listProjectUnits)**
9. **[Send Units to the Job](#operation/sendUnitsToJob)**
10. **[Launch & Resume/Run job](#operation/launchJob)**
11. **[Pause Job](#operation/pauseJob)**
12. **[Download Reports](#operation/downloadCsvFile)**
